articles.full_text:
Heavy rain batters Rajasthan:3 trains cancelled, red alert for 2 districts; rain continues in 5 districts, lightning kills 2 in Bhilwara
Heavy rainfall continues across Rajasthan. Since Friday morning (24 July), intermittent showers have been reported in Alwar, Rajsamand, Kotputli-Behror, Tonk and Jalore.
Heavy rain in Maharashtra and Gujarat has also affected railway traffic in Rajasthan. Three trains on the Mumbai route have been cancelled.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a red alert for heavy rainfall in Banswara and Dungarpur today. An orange alert has been issued for nine districts, while the remaining districts are under a yellow alert.
Earlier, on Thursday, several districts witnessed heavy rainfall. In Bhilwara's Raipur police station area, a grandmother and her granddaughter were killed after being struck by lightning in a village.
Meanwhile, Lasadiya in Udaipur recorded more than 11 inches of rainfall in the last 24 hours. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the spell of heavy rain is expected to continue until 27 July.
Lightning strikes amid heavy rain: Several parts of Bhilwara district received heavy rain on Thursday evening. During the downpour, an elderly shepherd, Dharma Nath, and his 11-year-old granddaughter, Gayatri Nath, were killed after being struck by lightning in Kalal Khedi village. Seven goats were also killed in the incident. Meanwhile, heavy rainfall in Maharashtra and Gujarat has disrupted rail services, affecting three trains to Mumbai from Jodhpur and Jaisalmer.
Rivers in spate: Heavy rainfall has begun affecting normal life across Rajasthan. Farmlands have been inundated in Hanumangarh, Sri Ganganagar, Beawar, Bhilwara, Udaipur and several other areas. The Som and Mahi rivers are flowing above danger levels in Dungarpur. Waterlogging, swollen seasonal rivers and the collapse of a culvert have cut off road connectivity to several villages and small towns.
Rainfall recorded: Bagor in Bhilwara recorded 60 mm of rainfall, Hamirgarh 50 mm, Salumber near Udaipur 61 mm, Jhalara 62 mm, Pachpadra in Barmer 51 mm, Patodi 55 mm, Amet in Rajsamand 61 mm, Sardargarh 54 mm, Rajsamand city 49 mm, Rashmi in Chittorgarh 53 mm, Bhadesar 45 mm, and Jalore 51 mm.
Heavy rain likely to continue for four more days: Jaipur Meteorological Centre Director Radheshyam Sharma said that three different weather systems are currently active over Rajasthan, making widespread rainfall likely across the state over the next four days. A shear zone has developed over southern Rajasthan, where westerly winds and easterly winds from the Bay of Bengal are converging. The combined effect of these systems is expected to keep the monsoon fully active across the state for the next four days.
Rajsamand district has been witnessing intermittent rainfall for the past two days.
Heavy rain lashed Alwar city and nearby areas on Friday morning. Following the downpour, waterfalls began cascading down the hills of Bala Quila.
